28dbbc5a312d7888db721bb7fbfdf4695e7f9e75.response: {"insights":[{"text":"The decision to replace `phoenix_tracing` with `yoyo_lazy_import` as the primary footgun is based on the validation that `yoyo_lazy_import` consistently triggered in the control group, while `phoenix_tracing` did not, ensuring the experiment's integrity.","scope":"repo","category":"decision"},{"text":"The gotcha of relying on `umap_neighbors` as a gating footgun was highlighted by its variance in performance across trials, necessitating its demotion to a non-gating cost signal to avoid ambiguous results in future experiments.","scope":"repo","category":"gotcha"},{"text":"The convention established is that all footgun tasks must be validated through empirical testing before being included in gating decisions, ensuring that only reliable constructs are used in critical experimental setups.","scope":"repo","category":"convention"},{"text":"The approach of using `phoenix_tracing` as a footgun was explicitly rejected due to its failure to exhibit the desired behavior in the control group, which was confirmed during the dogfood experiment.","scope":"repo","category":"rejected"}]}
